title: Spotlight Search
description: Learn to use Spotlight to quickly find files and open apps.

steps.instruction: Press Cmd+Space to open Spotlight Search. This is one of the most useful shortcuts on macOS!
steps.hint: Hold the Command (⌘) key and press the Space bar.

steps.instruction: Type 'Terminal' in the Spotlight search field to find the Terminal app.
steps.hint: Just start typing 'Terminal' in the search box that appeared.

steps.instruction: Click on Terminal in the results or press Return to open it.
steps.hint: Click the Terminal app in the search results, or press Enter/Return.

steps.instruction: Open Spotlight again with Cmd+Space and search for 'Notes' to find the Notes.txt file.
Click on the Notes.txt result to open it in TextEdit.
steps.hint: Click on Notes.txt in the Spotlight results.
